name: Texas Sushi
recipeIngredient: 1 pound lean, rare roast beef, cut into 1/8-1/4 inch slices
1 green bell pepper, cut into 3/8 inch strips
1 red bell pepper, cut into 3/8 inch strips
1 red bell pepper, cut into 3/8 inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Place rice and water in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer covered for 20 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed.
2. While the rice is cooking, combine vinegar, sugar, cumin, parsley, salt, and pepper in a separate saucepan. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. When the sugar dissolves, remove from heat. Stir the vinegar mixture into the cooked rice and set aside until the liquid is absorbed.
3. When the rice mixture has cooled, add cheese and stir. Adjust seasonings if needed.
4. To assemble: Cut roast beef into 1 1/2-inch wide by 3-inch long rectangles and marinate in salsa for 5-10 minutes. Drain and pat dry. Combine refried beans and cumin in a food processor and blend until smooth. Spread the beef slices with the bean mixture.
5. Top with a layer of rice filling and place strips of peppers (jalapenos can be added if desired) in a vertical line across the rice. Sprinkle with cilantro.
6. Roll, jellyroll fashion, and refrigerate until firm, at least 1 1/2 hours.
7. Before serving, cut each roll into 3-inch pieces. Tie each sushi with a chive.
8. Serve with salsa or guacamole. Yields 20 hors d'oeuvres.
